equilibrium.booting_pyodide: Initializing Pyodide in the browser (first run takes ~10–20s)…

run_status.skeleton_hint: Assigning initial demand under free flow — data will appear as soon as the first iteration completes.

sandbox.city_figure_desc: Linear city of {{length}} km with {{cells}} cells. CBD at center. Bar height and color scale with travel time on {{mode}}.

hero.title: The linear city
hero.subtitle: Mode share by location · 0 km = west edge · center = CBD

hints.demand_body: Each cell has three strata (High / Mid / Low). Active workers choose a mode via a multinomial logit over expected times and costs.
hints.supply_body: Cars use a BPR delay curve; bike lanes use a similar volume/capacity function; metro has endogenous frequency on the critical link with wait amplified by saturation.
hints.equilibrium_body: MSA iterations alternate demand ↔ supply until travel times stabilize. Convergence is shown in the residual figure.

land_use.title: Land use
land_use.subtitle: Alonso-Muth-Mills model
land_use.info_standalone: Solves the Alonso equilibrium with T = |i − CBD| (no transport).
land_use.info_coupled: Iterates land use ↔ transport until T stabilizes.
land_use.density_ignored_hint: In coupled mode, per-cell population comes from the land-use assignment (Σ H = {{total}}); the Sandbox's «Density per cell» slider is ignored.
land_use.outer_iter_hint: Each iteration runs the full transport MSA. Keep small in class.
land_use.run_placeholder: Run the solver to see the spatial assignment.
land_use.heading_distribution: Spatial distribution of households by stratum
land_use.solver_frechet_warn: Frechét is inconsistent with heterogeneous λ_h. See docs.

compare.title: Scenario comparison
compare.subtitle: Load up to 4 configurations in parallel. The first with a result is the baseline; others show deltas.

scenario_card.no_config: No configuration. Load a file or use the current sandbox.

compare.flow_no_profile: Mode «{{mode}}» has no per-cell demand profile.

coupled.title: Interdependence
coupled.lede: Transport and land use are not independent systems. Congestion reshuffles households; the new locations change demand; demand reshapes the network. This page lets you run a joint scenario and compare the naive equilibrium (no feedback) with the coupled one (with feedback).
coupled.placeholder: Select a scenario and press «Run scenario».
coupled.without_feedback_hint: First pass of the coupled loop: land use is solved with travel times equal to distance, unaware of congestion.
coupled.with_feedback_hint: Reconciled equilibrium: land and transport iterate mutually until stable. Differences reveal the feedback effect.

compact_toll.title: Compact + Road Pricing
compact_toll.desc: Dense city with congestion pricing. Classic European scenario.

sparse_proauto.title: Sparse + Pro-Auto
sparse_proauto.desc: Sprawled city with abundant road infra. US scenario.

base_probici.title: Base + Pro-Bike
base_probici.desc: Medium city with wide bike lanes and disincentivized parking.

compact_metro.title: Compact + Max Metro
compact_metro.desc: Dense city with maximum metro frequency and many stations.

interp.segregation_up: Feedback increased segregation by Δ={{delta}}. Congestion pushes high-time-value strata to the center; low-value ones migrate to the periphery.
interp.segregation_down: Feedback reduced segregation by Δ={{delta}}. Spatial reshuffle diluted the initial stratum concentration.
interp.welfare_regressive: Regressive effect: high-income gained {{alto}} while low-income gained {{bajo}}. Policy benefits those already better-off.
interp.welfare_progressive: Progressive effect: low-income gained {{bajo}} vs high-income {{alto}}. Policy favors the vulnerable more.
interp.accessibility_body: High gained {{alto}}, low {{bajo}} (ratio {{ratio}}×). Asymmetric distribution of CBD access.
interp.stable_body: Outer iterations did not meaningfully shift aggregate metrics. Feedback was weak in this scenario — try a more aggressive policy.

coupled_metrics.segregation_hint: Theil index. 0 = integrated city, 1 = perfect segregation. Drops when policy brings strata together; rises if the market pulls them apart.
coupled_metrics.welfare_hint: Average chosen-mode utility per stratum. Rises under policies that reduce time or cost for the average agent.
coupled_metrics.accessibility_hint: Hansen index per stratum: Σ exp(−α·T). Larger = destinations closer in generalized cost. Compare deltas, not absolute values.
